articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

article.headline: Che Adams nets hat trick as Scotland rout Liechtenstein
article.description: Ché Adams completed his hat trick in stoppage time as Scotland earned a 4-0 win over Liechtenstein in a friendly on Monday.
article.linkText: Adams nets hat trick as Scotland rout Liechtenstein

images.name: Che Adams [1296x729]
images.caption: Che Adams's hat trick helped Scotland to a comfortable win over Liechtenstein.
images.credit: Craig Williamson/SNS Group via Getty Images

peers.name: Che Adams [1296x1296]
peers.caption: Che Adams's hat trick helped Scotland to a comfortable win over Liechtenstein.
peers.credit: Craig Williamson/SNS Group via Getty Images

peers.name: Che Adams [1296x729]

peers.credit: Craig Williamson/SNS Group via Getty Images

peers.credit: Craig Williamson/SNS Group via Getty Images

peers.name: Che Adams [1296x864]
peers.caption: Che Adams's hat trick helped Scotland to a comfortable win over Liechtenstein.
peers.credit: Craig Williamson/SNS Group via Getty Images

peers.name: Che Adams [1296x518]
peers.caption: Che Adams's hat trick helped Scotland to a comfortable win over Liechtenstein.
peers.credit: Craig Williamson/SNS Group via Getty Images

article.story:
Ché Adams completed his hat trick in stoppage time as Scotland earned a 4-0 win over Liechtenstein in a friendly on Monday.

Adams took just four minutes to open the scoring, picking up on a loose ball in the opposition's box before sending it flying past goalkeeper Benjamin Büchel with a ferovious strike.

"That's one thing we spoke about in the dressing room, getting a fast start," Scotland boss Steve Clarke said.

He doubled Scotland's lead later in the first half with a tidy finish into the bottom left corner.

The result was put into little doubt when George Hirst got on the end of a cross at the back post to net Scotland's third before Adams nodded home to complete his hat trick with a just seconds remaining.

"It's been a while since the strikers scored," Clarke added.

"I spoke to both of them before the game. I said, if ever there was a game where they could both play up top, this was it. If ever there was a game where they could both get on the scoresheet, this was it."

The result come after Scotland lost 3-1 to Iceland in a friendly on Friday. Their next game is a 2026 World Cup qualifier scheduled for Sept. 5 against Denmark.

Goal! Liechtenstein 0, Scotland 1. Ché Adams (Scotland) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner following a corner.

Simon Lüchinger (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, Liechtenstein 0, Scotland 2.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Justin Ospelt replaces Benjamin Büchel.
Goal! Liechtenstein 0, Scotland 3. George Hirst (Scotland) left footed shot from very close range to the top left corner. Assisted by Ché Adams.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Livio Meier replaces Kenny Kindle.
